---
title: "Clinical Features"
author: "alexander-gysin (alexander.gysin@usz.ch)"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output: workflowr::wflow_html
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---
```{r setup_boxes, include=FALSE}
source(here::here("code", "00_config.R"))
```

# Introduction
This script represents Phase 1b of the pipeline. It takes the technically synchronized data from Phase 1a and performs rigorous biological feature engineering, ordinal mapping, and statistical Quality Control. It addresses extreme skewness and class imbalance, generating a finalized curated_clinical_spine.rds and a clinical_data_dictionary.rds for all downstream omics scripts.

# Setup and Import
`r what_this_does("Loaded required utility packages including readxl for the master mapping file.", "Established dynamic output directory paths to safely save curated data.", "Loaded the raw_filtered_spine generated by Script 01a.")`

```{r setup, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Packages
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
library(here)
library(kableExtra)
library(patchwork)
library(readxl) # Added to read the Master Mapping File
library(stringr)

# Knitr Config
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

# Output paths
current_file <- "01b_clinical_feature_engineering"
output_dir_data <- here::here("output", current_file)
if (!dir.exists(output_dir_data)) dir.create(output_dir_data, recursive = TRUE)

# Input paths
file_paths <- list(
  master_mapping_xlsx = "clinical_master_mapping.xlsx" 
)
mapping_path <- here::here("data", file_paths$master_mapping_xlsx)

# Load Technically Synced Data from 01a
raw_spine <- readRDS(here::here("output", "01a_data_import_and_qc", "raw_filtered_spine.rds"))
```

# Configuration
`r what_this_does("Established path to the Master Mapping Excel file.", "Set strict mathematical thresholds for QA/QC, including a parameterized IQR multiplier for outlier detection.")`
```{r configuration}
# 1. Define standard ordinal hierarchies
ordinal_map <- list(
  "Binary (no=0, yes=1)" = c("no" = "0", "yes" = "1"),
  "Temporal (never=0, prev=1, current=2)" = c("never" = "0", "previously" = "1", "current" = "2"),
  "Severity (none=0, rare=1, mod=2, ext=3)" = c("none" = "0", "rare" = "1", "moderate" = "2", "extensive" = "3"),
  "Roman Numerals (I=1, II=2, III=3)" = c("1" = "1", "i" = "1", "ii" = "2", "iii" = "3")
)

# 2. QC Mathematical Thresholds 
qc_config <- list(
  max_skewness = 2,        
  min_minority_pct = 0.05, 
  outlier_iqr_multiplier = 3.0, 
  manual_log_rejects = c(),
  log_offsets = list(
    default = 1,
    "CRP" = 2,
    "CK value in serum" = 1.1,
    "Creatinine" = 2,
    "NT-proBNP (pg/ml)" = 1.1,
    "Uric Acid (mg/dl)" = 2,
    "Min SpO2 (Excersise)" = 0.1
    # You can add specific offsets here if needed, e.g., CRP = 0.5
  ),
  olink_date = as.Date("2026-03-30")
)
  # Variables to bypass QC:
qc_bypass_conf <- c("cohort_group", "Subject_ID", "inCentraxx", "Geburtsdatum", "Geschlecht", "Entnahmedatum", "Entnahmedatum_Clean", "Birth_Year")

# Clinical variables to remove at the end
vars_to_remove = c("Geburtsdatum", "Birth_Year", "Geschlecht")

```

# Schema & Mapping Enforcement
`r analysis_decision("Single Source of Truth: All variable renaming, exclusion dropping, and datatype coercion is now dictated entirely by an external Excel file (clinical_master_mapping.xlsx).", "Rename First Rule: Variables are renamed immediately upon import. All subsequent downstream engineering relies exclusively on the 'Clean_Name'.", "Global Lowercase: All textual and categorical variables are swept to lowercase to definitively eliminate capitalization artifacts (e.g., 'Yes' vs 'yes').")`

`r what_this_does("Loaded the Master Mapping Excel file.", "Executed a 'Pre-Flight Check' to identify raw variables missing from the dictionary.", "Renamed all variables to their Clean_Name.", "Dropped all variables tagged with 'x'.", "Coerced remaining columns into pure Numerics (n), Categorical Factors (c), or Dates (d).", "Forced all character columns to lowercase.")`

```{r schema_enforcement, results='asis'}
# Initialize the global audit log to track every change made to the dataset
audit_log <- c("Phase 1: Schema & Mapping Audit Log\n")
curated_spine <- copy(raw_spine)

# 1. Load Master Mapping File
if (!file.exists(mapping_path)) stop("FATAL: clinical_master_mapping.xlsx not found.")
mapping_df <- read_excel(mapping_path)

# 2. Pre-Flight Check (Detect Unmapped Variables)
raw_cols <- names(curated_spine)
mapped_cols <- mapping_df$Raw_Name
unmapped <- setdiff(raw_cols, mapped_cols)

if(length(unmapped) > 0) {
  audit_log <- c(audit_log, sprintf("- **WARNING:** %d columns in raw data are missing from the mapping file.", length(unmapped)), setdiff(colnames(raw_spine), mapping_df$Raw_Name))
}

# 3. Rename First, Forget the Past
# Create a named vector: c("Old_Name" = "Clean_Name")
rename_dict <- setNames(mapping_df$Clean_Name, mapping_df$Raw_Name)
setnames(curated_spine, old = names(rename_dict), new = as.character(rename_dict), skip_absent = TRUE)

audit_log <- c(audit_log, sprintf("- Applied Master Naming Dictionary. Renamed %d variables. All subsequent steps now use 'Clean_Name'.", length(rename_dict)))

# 4. The Purge (Drop 'x' variables)
drop_vars <- mapping_df %>% filter(Data_Type == "x") %>% pull(Clean_Name)
actual_drops <- intersect(drop_vars, names(curated_spine))

if(length(actual_drops) > 0) {
  curated_spine[, (actual_drops) := NULL]
  audit_log <- c(audit_log, sprintf("- **Dropped %d variables** designated as 'x' (Excluded) in master mapping.", length(actual_drops)))
}

# 5. Safe Type Coercion
# Extract the target column names based on their flags
num_vars  <- mapping_df %>% filter(Data_Type == "n") %>% pull(Clean_Name) %>% intersect(names(curated_spine))
cat_vars  <- mapping_df %>% filter(Data_Type == "c") %>% pull(Clean_Name) %>% intersect(names(curated_spine))
date_vars <- mapping_df %>% filter(Data_Type == "d") %>% pull(Clean_Name) %>% intersect(names(curated_spine))

# Define helper functions to prevent R from crashing on weird text
safe_numeric <- function(x) { suppressWarnings(as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", trimws(as.character(x))))) }
safe_excel_date <- function(x) { suppressWarnings(as.Date(as.numeric(as.character(x)), origin = "1899-12-30")) }

# Apply the coercions efficiently using data.table syntax
if(length(num_vars) > 0)  curated_spine[, (num_vars)  := lapply(.SD, safe_numeric), .SDcols = num_vars]
if(length(cat_vars) > 0)  curated_spine[, (cat_vars)  := lapply(.SD, as.factor), .SDcols = cat_vars]
if(length(date_vars) > 0) curated_spine[, (date_vars) := lapply(.SD, safe_excel_date), .SDcols = date_vars]

audit_log <- c(audit_log, sprintf("- Enforced Datatypes: Coerced **%d Numeric**, **%d Categorical**, and **%d Date** variables.", length(num_vars), length(cat_vars), length(date_vars)))

# 6. Global Lowercase Sweep
char_cols <- names(curated_spine)[sapply(curated_spine, function (x) is.character(x) || is.factor(x))]
char_cols <- setdiff(char_cols, c("Subject_ID", date_vars))

if (length(char_cols) > 0) {
  curated_spine[, (char_cols) := lapply(.SD, tolower), .SDcols = char_cols]
  audit_log <- c(audit_log, sprintf("- Standardized %d categorical/character variables to lowercase.", length(char_cols)))
}

# Convert categoricals to factors after lowering
if(length(cat_vars) > 0) curated_spine[, (cat_vars) := lapply(.SD, as.factor), .SDcols = cat_vars]

# Print the Phase 1 Log
cat(paste(audit_log, collapse = "\n\n"))
```

# Feature Engineering
`r what_this_does("Engineered cohort groups strictly from Subject_ID prefixes.", "Calculated standard baseline demographics (Age, Sex, Sample_Age) and temporal deltas between clinical assessments and blood draws.", "Permanently encoded Subsets (Diffuse/Limited/Sine) as One-Hot binary variables.", "Extracted individual medications from grouped text strings into discrete binary features.")`

```{r feature_engineering, results='asis'}
audit_log <- c("Phase 2: Feature Engineering Audit Log\n")

# 1. Cohort Group Assignment
curated_spine[, cohort_group := fcase(
  str_detect(Subject_ID, "^Nonact"), GRP_NON_ACTIVE,
  str_detect(Subject_ID, "^Act"),    GRP_ACTIVE,
  str_detect(Subject_ID, "^Ctrl"),   GRP_CONTROL,
  default = NA_character_
)]
curated_spine[, cohort_group := factor(cohort_group, levels = c(GRP_ACTIVE, GRP_NON_ACTIVE, GRP_CONTROL))]

# 2. Demographics (Age and Sample Age)
# Note: Relies on the standard names in your mapping file
if (all(c("Entnahmedatum", "Geburtsdatum") %in% names(curated_spine))) {
  curated_spine[, Entnahmedatum_Clean := Entnahmedatum]
  curated_spine[, Birth_Year := as.numeric(trimws(Geburtsdatum))]
  curated_spine[, Age := year(Entnahmedatum_Clean) - Birth_Year]
  curated_spine[, Sample_Age := as.numeric(difftime(qc_config$olink_date, Entnahmedatum_Clean, units = "days")) / 365.25]
  audit_log <- c(audit_log, "- Calculated 'Age' and 'Sample_Age' using Entnahmedatum and Geburtsdatum.")
}

# 3. Sex Normalization
if ("Geschlecht" %in% names(curated_spine)) {
  curated_spine[, Sex := fcase(
    Geschlecht == "weiblich" | tolower(Geschlecht) == "female", SEX_FEMALE, 
    Geschlecht == "männlich" | tolower(Geschlecht) == "male", SEX_MALE,   
    default = NA_character_
  )]
  curated_spine[, Sex := factor(Sex, levels = c(SEX_MALE, SEX_FEMALE))]
  audit_log <- c(audit_log, "- Normalized 'Geschlecht' into standardized factor 'Sex'.")
}

# 4. Dynamic Date Deltas (Using the dictionary's 'd' tags)
# Find all columns tagged as dates, excluding the base reference dates
assessment_dates <- setdiff(date_vars, c("Visit Date", "Entnahmedatum", "Einlagerungsdatum", "Geburtsdatum"))

if (length(assessment_dates) > 0 && "Entnahmedatum_Clean" %in% names(curated_spine)) {
  for (d_col in assessment_dates) {
    # Strip procedural text to create a clean prefix for the delta column
    clean_prefix <- gsub(".*Date of assessment for |.*Date of |.*date of ", "", d_col)
    clean_name <- paste0("Delta_", gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", clean_prefix), "_Years")
    curated_spine[, (clean_name) := as.numeric(difftime(get(d_col), Entnahmedatum_Clean, units = "days")) / 365.25]
  }
  audit_log <- c(audit_log, sprintf("- Calculated %d temporal Delta variables against blood draw date.", length(assessment_dates)))
}



# 5. Medication Re-engineering (Explosion & One-Hot)
med_cols <- c("Immunosuppressants", "Major vascular", "Steroids", "Other_medication")
actual_med_cols <- intersect(med_cols, names(curated_spine))

if (length(actual_med_cols) > 0) {
  all_drugs <- c()
  
  # Pass 1: Extract universe of unique drugs
  for (m in actual_med_cols) {
    vec <- as.character(curated_spine[[m]])
    vec <- vec[!is.na(vec) & vec != "" & vec != "no" & vec != "none"]
    split_drugs <- unlist(strsplit(vec, "[,;]"))
    clean_drugs <- trimws(split_drugs)
    clean_drugs <- clean_drugs[clean_drugs != "yes" & clean_drugs != "no" & clean_drugs != "" & clean_drugs != "none"]
    all_drugs <- unique(c(all_drugs, clean_drugs))
  }

  # Pass 2: Create binary columns for specific drugs
  for (drug in all_drugs) {
    col_name <- paste0("Med_", gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", drug))
    has_drug <- apply(curated_spine[, ..actual_med_cols], 1, function(row) {
      any(grepl(paste0("\\b", regex(drug), "\\b"), row, ignore.case = TRUE))
    })
    curated_spine[, (col_name) := factor(ifelse(has_drug, "yes", "no"), levels = c("no", "yes"))]
  }

  # Pass 3: Overwrite parent columns to clean yes/no
  for (m in actual_med_cols) {
    vec <- as.character(curated_spine[[m]])
    is_yes <- !is.na(vec) & vec != "" & vec != "no" & vec != "none"
    curated_spine[, (m) := factor(ifelse(is_yes, "yes", "no"), levels = c("no", "yes"))]
  }
  
  audit_log <- c(audit_log, sprintf("- Parsed medication strings: Extracted %d specific drugs into one-hot variables and binarized parent classes.", length(all_drugs)))
}

# 6. Biological Failsafes
if("CRP" %in% names(curated_spine)) curated_spine[CRP <= 0, CRP := NA_real_]

#7. Official Eustar active/inactive
if ("eustarAI" %in% names(curated_spine)) {
  curated_spine[, eustar_group := fcase(
    eustarAI >= 2.5, "Active", 
    eustarAI < 2.5, "Inactive",   
    default = NA_character_
  )]
  curated_spine[, eustar_group := factor(eustar_group, levels = c("Active", "Inactive"))]
  audit_log <- c(audit_log, "- Created 'eustar_group' based on eustarAI 2.5 cutoff.")
}

# Remove redundant clinical variables
curated_spine <- curated_spine %>%
  select(-all_of(vars_to_remove))

cat(paste(audit_log, collapse = "\n\n"))
```


# Numeric Spine Generation
`r what_this_does("Generated a parallel curated_spine_numeric by mapping severity rankings (e.g., 'none', 'rare', 'moderate') into mathematical ordinal scales (0-3).", "Explicitly binarized the Sex and Cohort Group variables.", "Output a visual tracker to audit exactly which mappings were applied to which variables.")`

```{r numeric_spine_generation, results='asis'}
curated_spine_numeric <- copy(curated_spine)

ordinal_log_df <- data.frame(Variable = character(), Mappings_Applied = character(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
scan_cols <- names(curated_spine)[sapply(curated_spine, function(x) is.character(x) || is.factor(x))]

for (col in scan_cols) {
  vec <- tolower(as.character(curated_spine[[col]]))
  changed <- FALSE
  map_applied <- c()
  
  # Check against all hierarchical dictionaries
  for (map_name in names(ordinal_map)) {
    mapping <- ordinal_map[[map_name]]
    if (any(vec %in% names(mapping), na.rm = TRUE)) {
      vec <- unname(mapping[vec]) %>% coalesce(vec)
      changed <- TRUE
      map_applied <- c(map_applied, map_name)
    }
  }
  
  if (changed) {
    curated_spine_numeric[[col]] <- as.numeric(vec)
    ordinal_log_df <- rbind(ordinal_log_df, data.frame(Variable = col, Mappings_Applied = paste(map_applied, collapse = " | ")))
    
  } else if (col == "Sex") {
    curated_spine_numeric[[col]] <- ifelse(curated_spine[[col]] == SEX_FEMALE, 1, 0)
    ordinal_log_df <- rbind(ordinal_log_df, data.frame(Variable = col, Mappings_Applied = "Explicit Binary (Male=0, Female=1)"))
  
  } else if (col == "eustar_group") {
    curated_spine_numeric[[col]] <- ifelse(curated_spine[[col]] == "Active", 1, 0)
    ordinal_log_df <- rbind(ordinal_log_df, data.frame(Variable = col, Mappings_Applied = "Explicit Binary eustar_group (Active=1, Inactive=0)"))
      
  } else if (col == "cohort_group") {
    curated_spine_numeric[[col]] <- case_when(
      curated_spine[[col]] == GRP_CONTROL ~ 0,
      curated_spine[[col]] == GRP_NON_ACTIVE ~ 1,
      curated_spine[[col]] == GRP_ACTIVE ~ 2,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
    ordinal_log_df <- rbind(ordinal_log_df, data.frame(Variable = col, Mappings_Applied = "Explicit Ordinal (Control=0, Non-Active=1, Active=2)"))
  }
}

# Failsafe: Force any remaining text columns that only contain numbers into pure numerics
curated_spine_numeric <- curated_spine_numeric %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ {
    if((is.character(.x) || is.factor(.x)) && all(grepl("^[0-9.]+$", na.omit(as.character(.x))))) {
      as.numeric(as.character(.x))
    } else {
      .x
    }
  }))
setDT(curated_spine_numeric)

# Print Tracker Table
if(nrow(ordinal_log_df) > 0) {
  ord_tbl <- ordinal_log_df %>%
    arrange(Mappings_Applied, Variable) %>%
    kableExtra::kbl(format = "html", row.names = FALSE, caption = "<b style='font-size:1.2em; color:#333;'>Ordinalization Tracker (Applied to Math Spine Only)</b>") %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = TRUE, position = "left") %>%
    kableExtra::collapse_rows(columns = 2, valign = "top") %>%
    kableExtra::scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "400px")
  if (interactive()) print(ord_tbl) else cat(as.character(ord_tbl))
}
```
# Automated QC & Data Dictionary
`r analysis_decision("Continuous variables were evaluated for both skewness and extreme outliers simultaneously.", "Skewed variables were transformed using the Natural Log: ln(x + c). Variables producing NaNs were flagged and skipped.", "Categorical variables with a minority class <10% were flagged as IMBALANCED.", "Character variables were deliberately bypassed from statistical QC.")`

`r what_this_does("Iterated through every clinical feature.", "Implemented an accumulating flag system (e.g., 'SKEWED & EXTREME OUTLIERS').", "Unified data taxonomy into a single, clean 'Class' column (Continuous, Ordinal, Categorical Binary/Nominal, Excluded).", "Merged statistical QC results with manual exclusion notes from the Master Mapping file to generate the final definitive Data Dictionary.")`

```{r QC_and_Dictionary}
calc_skew <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  n <- length(x)
  if (n < 3) return(NA_real_)
  sum((x - mean(x))^3 / n) / (sd(x)^3)
}

calc_outliers <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (length(x) < 5) return(0)
  q25 <- quantile(x, 0.25)
  q75 <- quantile(x, 0.75)
  iqr <- q75 - q25
  upper_bound <- q75 + (qc_config$outlier_iqr_multiplier * iqr)
  lower_bound <- q25 - (qc_config$outlier_iqr_multiplier * iqr)
  sum(x > upper_bound | x < lower_bound)
}

# Variables to bypass in the statistical scan
qc_bypass <- c(qc_bypass_conf, date_vars)
candidate_vars <- setdiff(names(curated_spine), qc_bypass)

dict_list <- list()
qc_plots_skew <- list()
imb_log_list <- list()

for (v in candidate_vars) {
  vec <- curated_spine[[v]]
  vec_patients <- vec[!is.na(curated_spine$cohort_group) & curated_spine$cohort_group != GRP_CONTROL]
  miss_pct <- sum(is.na(vec_patients)) / length(vec_patients)
  
  # A. CHARACTER / IDENTIFIER LOGIC
  if (is.character(vec)) {
    dict_list[[length(dict_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      Variable = v, Class = "Identifier / Text", Missing_Pct = miss_pct, 
      Distribution_Metric = "N/A (Free Text)", 
      QC_Flag = "PASS", Action_Taken = "Character bypassed QC"
    )
    next
    
  # B. NUMERIC / CONTINUOUS LOGIC
  } else if (is.numeric(vec)) {
    if (length(unique(na.omit(vec))) > 5) {
      skew_val <- calc_skew(vec_patients)
      extreme_outliers <- calc_outliers(vec_patients)
      
      flags <- c()
      if (extreme_outliers > 0) flags <- c(flags, "EXTREME OUTLIERS")
      if (!is.na(skew_val) && abs(skew_val) > qc_config$max_skewness) flags <- c(flags, "SKEWED")
      
      qc_flag <- if (length(flags) > 0) paste(flags, collapse = " & ") else "PASS"
      dist_metric <- sprintf("Skew: %.2f | Outliers: %d", skew_val, extreme_outliers)
      action <- if ("SKEWED" %in% flags) "Log10 Transformed" else if ("EXTREME OUTLIERS" %in% flags) "Flagged (Review for unit errors)" else "None"
      
      if ("SKEWED" %in% flags) {
        log_name <- gsub("_+$", "", paste0("log_", gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", v)))
        
        # Determine variable-specific offset
        offset <- if(!is.null(qc_config$log_offsets[[v]])) qc_config$log_offsets[[v]] else qc_config$log_offsets[["default"]]
        
        # Attempt mathematical transformation
        transformed_patients <- suppressWarnings(log(vec_patients + offset))
        
        if (any(is.nan(transformed_patients))) {
          cat(sprintf("\n\n> **WARNING:** Variable '%s' produced NaNs during ln(x + %g) transformation. Transformation aborted.\n\n", v, offset))
          action <- "Transformation Failed (NaN)"
          qc_flag <- paste(qc_flag, "FAILED LOG", sep=" & ")
        } else {
          curated_spine[, (log_name) := log(get(v) + offset)]
          curated_spine_numeric[, (log_name) := log(get(v) + offset)]
          
          log_patients <- curated_spine[[log_name]][!is.na(curated_spine$cohort_group) & curated_spine$cohort_group != GRP_CONTROL]
          log_skew <- calc_skew(log_patients)
          log_outliers <- calc_outliers(log_patients)
          
          log_flags <- c()
          if (log_name %in% qc_config$manual_log_rejects) {
            log_flags <- c(log_flags, "LOG REJECTED")
          } else {
            if (log_outliers > 0) log_flags <- c(log_flags, "EXTREME OUTLIERS")
            if (!is.na(log_skew) && abs(log_skew) > qc_config$max_skewness) log_flags <- c(log_flags, "SKEWED")
          }
          
          log_qc_flag <- if (length(log_flags) > 0) paste(log_flags, collapse = " & ") else "PASS"
          log_action  <- if ("LOG REJECTED" %in% log_flags) "Flagged by User" else "Engineered to fix skew"
          log_dist_metric <- sprintf("Skew: %.2f | Outliers: %d", log_skew, log_outliers)

          qc_plots_skew[[v]] <- local({
            plot_df <- data.frame(Raw = vec_patients, Transformed = log_patients)
            p_raw <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Raw)) + geom_histogram(fill = "#c0392b", color = "white", bins = 30) + theme_project_base(10) + labs(title = v, x = "Raw Data")
            p_log <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Transformed)) + geom_histogram(fill = "#2980b9", color = "white", bins = 30) + theme_project_base(10) + labs(title = log_name, x = sprintf("Ln(x + %g) Data", offset))
            p_raw | p_log
          })

          dict_list[[length(dict_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = log_name, Class = "Continuous", Missing_Pct = miss_pct, Distribution_Metric = log_dist_metric, QC_Flag = log_qc_flag, Action_Taken = log_action)
        }
      }
      
      dict_list[[length(dict_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = v, Class = "Continuous", Missing_Pct = miss_pct, Distribution_Metric = dist_metric, QC_Flag = qc_flag, Action_Taken = action)
    } else {
      # Numbers with <5 levels are Ordinal/Binary.
      dict_list[[length(dict_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = v, Class = "Ordinal / Binary", Missing_Pct = miss_pct, Distribution_Metric = "N/A (<5 Levels)", QC_Flag = "PASS", Action_Taken = "None")
    }
    
  # C. FACTOR / CATEGORICAL LOGIC
  } else if (is.factor(vec) || is.logical(vec)) {
    props <- prop.table(table(vec_patients))
    counts <- table(vec_patients)
    min_prop <- if(length(props) > 0) min(props) else 0
    qc_flag <- "PASS"
    action <- "None"
    
    if (length(props) < 2) {
      qc_flag <- "ZERO VARIANCE"
      action <- "Flagged"
    } else if (min_prop < qc_config$min_minority_pct) {
      qc_flag <- "IMBALANCED"
      action <- "Flagged"
      imb_log_list[[v]] <- data.frame(Variable = v, Level = as.character(names(props)), Count = as.numeric(counts), Percentage = as.numeric(props))
    }
    
    # NEW: Unified Class Assignment
    unified_class <- if(length(props) == 2) "Categorical (Binary)" else "Categorical (Nominal)"
    
    dict_list[[length(dict_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = v, Class = unified_class, Missing_Pct = miss_pct, Distribution_Metric = sprintf("Minority: %.1f%%", min_prop * 100), QC_Flag = qc_flag, Action_Taken = action)
  }
}

# Construct Dictionary
base_dict <- bind_rows(dict_list) %>% mutate(Exclusion_Reason = "None")

# Pull dropped variables directly from the Master Mapping File!
drops_dict <- mapping_df %>%
  filter(Data_Type == "x") %>%
  transmute(
    Variable = Clean_Name,
    Class = "Excluded",
    Missing_Pct = 1, 
    Distribution_Metric = "N/A",
    QC_Flag = "EXCLUDED",
    Action_Taken = "Dropped via Mapping",
    Exclusion_Reason = Exclusion_Reason 
  )

# Calculate missing % and order data dictionary
data_dictionary <- bind_rows(base_dict, drops_dict) %>%
  mutate(
    Missing_Pct = sprintf("%.1f%%", as.numeric(Missing_Pct) * 100), 
    Sort_Order = case_when(
      QC_Flag == "EXCLUDED" ~ 1,
      grepl("ZERO VARIANCE", QC_Flag) ~ 2, 
      grepl("IMBALANCED", QC_Flag) ~ 3, 
      grepl("OUTLIERS", QC_Flag) ~ 4, 
      grepl("LOG REJECTED", QC_Flag) ~ 5, 
      grepl("SKEWED", QC_Flag) ~ 6, 
      QC_Flag == "PASS" ~ 7, 
      TRUE ~ 8
    )
  ) %>% 
  arrange(Sort_Order, Variable) %>% 
  select(-Sort_Order)

# Final Column Ordering
core_cols <- intersect(c("Subject_ID", "cohort_group", "Age", "Sex", "Sample_Age"), names(curated_spine))
setcolorder(curated_spine, core_cols)
setcolorder(curated_spine_numeric, core_cols)
```

## QC Diagnostics {.tabset}

### Skewness Corrections
The following continuous variables violated the Skewness threshold (|Skew| > 2.5). The algorithm automatically engineered a normalized log_ variant.

```{r QC_diagnostics, results='asis'}
if (length(qc_plots_skew) > 0) {
  for (v in names(qc_plots_skew)) {
    cat(sprintf("#### %s\n\n", v))
    print(qc_plots_skew[[v]])
    cat("\n\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("> No severe skewness detected.\n\n")
}
```

### Extreme Outliers
The following continuous variables contain extreme outliers (values exceeding the parameterized IQR multiplier). These have been flagged for manual review to ensure they are not data entry or unit errors.

```{r qc_outliers, results='asis'}
# Query the dictionary for variables containing the outlier flag (handles compound flags)
outlier_vars <- data_dictionary %>% 
  filter(grepl("EXTREME OUTLIERS", QC_Flag)) %>% 
  pull(Variable)

if (length(outlier_vars) > 0) {
  for (v in outlier_vars) {
    cat(sprintf("#### %s\n\n", v))
    
    vec_patients <- curated_spine[[v]][!is.na(curated_spine$cohort_group) & curated_spine$cohort_group != GRP_CONTROL]
    
    if (sum(!is.na(vec_patients)) < 5) {
      cat("> Insufficient data to plot bounds.\n\n")
      next
    }
    
    plot_df <- data.frame(Value = vec_patients)
    
    q25 <- quantile(vec_patients, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
    q75 <- quantile(vec_patients, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
    iqr <- q75 - q25
    upper_bound <- q75 + (qc_config$outlier_iqr_multiplier * iqr)
    lower_bound <- q25 - (qc_config$outlier_iqr_multiplier * iqr)
    
    p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Value)) + 
      geom_histogram(fill = "#e67e22", color = "white", bins = 40) + 
      geom_vline(xintercept = c(lower_bound, upper_bound), color = "#c0392b", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
      theme_project_base(10) + 
      labs(title = v, 
           subtitle = sprintf("Red dashed lines indicate %gx IQR thresholds [%.1f, %.1f]", qc_config$outlier_iqr_multiplier, lower_bound, upper_bound), 
           x = "Data Value", y = "Count")
    
    print(p)
    cat("\n\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("> No extreme outliers detected.\n\n")
}
```

### Class Imbalances
The following categorical variables have a minority class of < 10%. They are retained in the dataset for Table 1 summaries, but flagged in the Dictionary to protect downstream omics models from zero-variance crashes.
```{r class_imbalances, results='asis'}
if (length(imb_log_list) > 0) {
  
  # Bind the list and calculate which specific rows triggered the flag
  imb_df <- bind_rows(imb_log_list) %>%
    mutate(
      Is_Offender = Percentage < qc_config$min_minority_pct,
      Percentage = sprintf("%.1f%%", Percentage * 100)
    ) %>%
    arrange(Variable, desc(Count)) # Sort levels by largest to smallest within each variable
  
  imb_tbl <- imb_df %>%
    select(Variable, Level, Count, Percentage) %>%
    kableExtra::kbl(format = "html", escape = FALSE, row.names = FALSE) %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = TRUE, position = "left") %>%
    # Group the variable names so the table is extremely easy to read
    kableExtra::collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") %>%
    # Highlight the offending minority classes in bold red!
    kableExtra::row_spec(which(imb_df$Is_Offender), bold = TRUE, color = "#c0392b") %>%
    kableExtra::scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "500px")
  
  if (interactive()) {
    print(imb_tbl)
  } else {
    cat(as.character(imb_tbl))
    cat("\n\n")
  }
  
} else {
  cat("> No severe class imbalances detected.\n\n")
}
```

# Clinical Domain Atlas
`r what_this_does("Iterates through the global clinical domain lists to construct a comprehensive visual atlas.", "Dynamically routes continuous variables to histograms and categorical variables to smart-sorted bar charts.", "Bypasses the gitignored output folder by saving images directly to docs/assets/ and generating interactive HTML dropdowns for web publishing.")`

<p>Select a clinical domain to visualize its feature distributions:</p>

```{r build_dropdown, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
# 1. Build the Dropdown
cat('<select id="atlasSelector" onchange="switchAtlasPlot()" class="form-control" style="width: auto; margin-bottom: 20px;">\n')
cat('  <option value="">-- Select Domain --</option>\n')

for(dom in names(clinical_domains)) {
  clean_label <- gsub("_", " ", dom)
  cat(sprintf('  <option value="%s">%s</option>\n', dom, clean_label))
}
cat('</select>\n\n')

# 2. Build the Image Container
cat('<div id="atlasPlotContainer">\n')
cat('  <img id="atlasImage" src="" style="width: 100%; display: none; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px;">\n')
cat('</div>\n\n')

# 3. Build the Javascript (Injecting the dynamic folder path)
cat('<script>\n')
cat('function switchAtlasPlot() {\n')
cat('  var val = document.getElementById("atlasSelector").value;\n')
cat('  var img = document.getElementById("atlasImage");\n')
cat('  if (val) {\n')
cat(sprintf('    img.src = "assets/%s/" + val + "_atlas.png";\n', current_file))
cat('    img.style.display = "block";\n')
cat('  } else {\n')
cat('    img.style.display = "none";\n')
cat('  }\n')
cat('}\n')
cat('</script>\n\n')
```

```{r clinical_domain_atlas, results='hide'}
# Point directly to the web-accessible docs folder
atlas_dir <- here::here("docs", "assets", current_file)
if(!dir.exists(atlas_dir)) dir.create(atlas_dir, recursive = TRUE)

for (dom in names(clinical_domains)) {
  
  vars <- intersect(clinical_domains[[dom]], names(curated_spine))
  vars <- setdiff(vars, c("Subject_ID", "cohort_group"))
  if(length(vars) == 0) next
  
  plot_list <- list()
  
  for (v in vars) {
    vec <- curated_spine[[v]]
    vec_clean <- vec[!is.na(vec) & !is.na(curated_spine$cohort_group) & curated_spine$cohort_group != GRP_CONTROL]
    
    qc_row <- data_dictionary %>% filter(Variable == v)
    qc_flag <- if(nrow(qc_row) > 0) qc_row$QC_Flag[1] else "UNKNOWN"
    miss_pct <- if(nrow(qc_row) > 0) qc_row$Missing_Pct[1] else "UNKNOWN"
    
    flag_color <- case_when(
      qc_flag == "PASS" ~ "#27ae60",
      grepl("OUTLIERS|IMBALANCED|ZERO|REJECTED", qc_flag) ~ "#c0392b",
      grepl("SKEWED", qc_flag) ~ "#2980b9",
      TRUE ~ "black"
    )
    
    if (is.numeric(vec_clean)) {
      med <- median(vec_clean, na.rm=TRUE)
      q25 <- quantile(vec_clean, 0.25, na.rm=TRUE)
      q75 <- quantile(vec_clean, 0.75, na.rm=TRUE)
      sub_txt <- sprintf("QC: %s\nDist: Med %.1f [%.1f - %.1f]\nMissing: %s", qc_flag, med, q25, q75, miss_pct)
      
      p <- ggplot(data.frame(x=vec_clean), aes(x)) +
        geom_histogram(fill="grey70", color="white", bins=30) +
        geom_vline(xintercept=med, color="#2c3e50", linetype="dashed", linewidth=1) +
        theme_project_base(10) +
        labs(title = v, subtitle = sub_txt, x=NULL, y=NULL) +
        theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(color = flag_color, face="bold", size=rel(0.85)),
              plot.title = element_text(size=rel(0.9), face="bold"))
              
    } else {
      val_lower <- as.character(vec_clean) # Already lowercased via the sweep!
      
      # SMART SORTING LOGIC: Ordinal vs Frequency
      if (all(val_lower %in% c("none", "rare", "moderate", "extensive"))) {
        plot_vec <- factor(val_lower, levels = c("none", "rare", "moderate", "extensive"))
      } else if (all(val_lower %in% c("never", "previously", "current"))) {
        plot_vec <- factor(val_lower, levels = c("never", "previously", "current"))
      } else if (all(val_lower %in% c("no", "yes"))) {
        plot_vec <- factor(val_lower, levels = c("no", "yes"))
      } else {
        plot_vec <- forcats::fct_infreq(as.character(vec_clean))
      }
      
      props <- prop.table(table(plot_vec)) * 100
      lvl_strs <- paste0(names(props), ": ", sprintf("%.0f%%", props))
      if(length(lvl_strs) > 3) lvl_strs <- c(lvl_strs[1:3], "...")
      
      sub_txt <- sprintf("QC: %s\nDist: %s\nMissing: %s", qc_flag, paste(lvl_strs, collapse=", "), miss_pct)
      
      p <- ggplot(data.frame(x=plot_vec), aes(x)) +
        geom_bar(fill="grey70", color="black", alpha=0.8) +
        theme_project_base(10) +
        labs(title = v, subtitle = sub_txt, x=NULL, y=NULL) +
        theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(color = flag_color, face="bold", size=rel(0.85)),
              plot.title = element_text(size=rel(0.9), face="bold"),
              axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1))
    }
    
    plot_list[[v]] <- p
  }
  
  grid_p <- wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 3) +
    plot_annotation(title = sprintf("Clinical Domain: %s", gsub("_", " ", dom)),
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size=18, face="bold")))
  
  dyn_h <- max(5, ceiling(length(plot_list) / 3) * 3 + 1)
  file_name <- paste0(dom, "_atlas.png")
  file_path <- file.path(atlas_dir, file_name)
  ggsave(file_path, grid_p, width=14, height=dyn_h, dpi=150, bg="white")
}
```

# Export
`r what_this_does("Exported the human-readable curated_clinical_spine.rds for visualization (Phase 2).", "Exported the binarized curated_clinical_spine_numeric.rds for machine learning and linear algebra (Phase 3).", "Exported the definitive clinical_data_dictionary.rds detailing the QC status.")`
```{r export}
saveRDS(curated_spine, file.path(output_dir_data, "curated_clinical_spine.rds"))
saveRDS(curated_spine_numeric, file.path(output_dir_data, "curated_clinical_spine_numeric.rds"))
saveRDS(data_dictionary, file.path(output_dir_data, "clinical_data_dictionary.rds"))

writexl::write_xlsx(curated_spine, file.path(output_dir_data, "curated_clinical_spine.xlsx"))

cat("Script 01b Complete!\n")
```
